revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:堀越神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Horikoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Horikoshi-jinja1.jpg |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|qid=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}} Chausuyama-chō 1-8 |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 39 |latitude_sec = 5.3 |latitude_dir = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 30 |longitude_sec = 47.6 |longitude_dir = E |country_code = JP |map = Japan Osaka#Osaka city |enshrined_deities = {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|qid=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Koteko|en|Ōtomo no Koteko|qid=Q8081770|lt=Empress Kotehime|1=Ōtomo no Koteko}}, {{ill|Prince Hachiko|en|Prince Hachiko|qid=Q7244045|lt=Prince Hachiko|1=Prince Hachiko}}, {{ill|Princess Nishikide|ja|錦代皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Nishikide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |shrine_Rank = Former Village Shrine |founded = |main_hall = |alternate_names = |pilgrimage_sites = |annual_festival = |rites = |location = {{ill|Q86727104|qid=Q86727104|1=Q86727104}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.65147222|N|135.51322222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Sushun|qid=Q349238|1=Emperor Sushun}} |part_of = {{ill|shitennōji shichimiya|qid=Q11419823|1=Shitennōji Shichimiya}} }}
'''Horikoshi Shrine''' (ほりこしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|qid=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It was historically classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.<ref name="naniwa">[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/naniwa/publication/book08.pdf#search='%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E3%82%92%E4%B8%AD%E5%BF%83%E3%81%A8%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B%E6%9D%91%E8%90%BD%E7%94%9F%E6%B4%BB%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A' Kansai University Naniwa Cultural Heritage Research Center, "Village Life Centered Around Shrines Report" (Part 1: Osaka Prefecture - Osaka City, Mishima District, Toyono District)], accessed November 20, 2018.</ref>
== History == It is said that {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} established Horikoshi Shrine while founding {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|qid=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}} to enshrine {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|qid=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}} at the scenic location of {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun (Osaka)|ja|茶臼山古墳 (大阪市)|lt=Chausuyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun (Osaka City)|qq=}}. The shrine is counted among the {{ill|Shitennō-ji Seven Shrines|ja|四天王寺七宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennō-ji Seven Shrines|1=Shitennōji Shichimiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11419823}}. It is believed that the shrine's name originates from a moat that once ran along the southern edge of the precincts until the mid-Meiji period. Worshippers crossed this moat to visit the shrine.<ref name="OSAKA-INFO">[https://osaka-info.jp/page/horikoshijinja Horikoshi Shrine | OSAKA-INFO], accessed November 20, 2018.</ref> The shrine has long been venerated for the belief that it "grants a once-in-a-lifetime wish."
During the Meiji period, Horikoshi Shrine was designated a village shrine.
Within the precincts lies the {{ill|Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine|ja|熊野第一王子之宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, originally the first of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Kumano Ninety-Nine Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3200280}} known as {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597673}}.
Today, while the shrine faces the busy {{ill|Tanimachi-suji Avenue|ja|谷町筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanimachi-suji Avenue|1=Tanimachi-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633070}}, its grounds retain an air of tranquility, with centuries-old trees and abundant greenery.<ref name="OSAKA-INFO" />
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|qid=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}}
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōtomo no Koteko|en|Ōtomo no Koteko|qid=Q8081770|lt=Empress Kotehime|1=Ōtomo no Koteko}} * {{ill|Prince Hachiko|en|Prince Hachiko|qid=Q7244045|lt=Prince Hachiko|1=Prince Hachiko}} * {{ill|Princess Nishikide|ja|錦代皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Nishikide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Grounds == * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Chausuyama {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} - Formerly located atop Chausuyama. During the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|en|Siege of Osaka|qid=Q475780|lt=Siege of Osaka|1=Siege of Osaka}} (Winter Campaign), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} prayed at the shrine. It is said that during the Summer Campaign, a white fox of Chausuyama Inari saved Ieyasu during an attack by {{ill|Sanada Yukimura|en|Sanada Yukimura|qid=Q3948081|lt=Sanada Yukimura|1=Sanada Yukimura}}. Following the Summer Campaign, the shrine was relocated to its current location. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q321242}} steward would visit the shrine upon assuming office, offering rituals and dedicating votive lanterns. * Shrine Office * Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine - Originally {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597673}}, the first shrine of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Shrines. It was once located near the western gate of Shitennō-ji as Kumano Shrine but was moved to its current location on February 10, 1915 (Taishō 4). * Tajo Shinsei Chintaku Reifu Sonjin * Black Dragon Shrine * White Dragon Shrine
== location == ; Address * Chausuyama-chō 1-8, Tennoji-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture ; Transportation * Nearest Station: {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|qid=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station|1=Tennōji Station}} on the {{ill|Midōsuji Line|en|Midōsuji Line|qid=Q1192413|lt=Osaka Metro Midōsuji Line|1=Midōsuji Line}}, approximately a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Horikoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.horikoshijinja.or.jp/ Horikoshi Shrine Official Website]
